english: - 4. tsad rgyág-pa (tshad rgyag pa) to guess, conjecture, suppose Sch., cf. tsod (tshod). - 5. sometimes for tsád-pa (tshad pa) heat; for tsad-ma (tshad ma) logic, dbu-tsád (dbu tshad) Madhyamika logic Tar. 179, 17, Schf.

I. sbst. 1. heat, in gen.; tsád-pa byuṅ-tse (tshad pa byung tse) when it grows hot Glr.; tsád-pas gduṅ-ba (tshad pas gdung ba) to be tormented by the heat Glr.; tsád-pas (tshad pas), or vulg. tsád-pa-nas (tshad pa nas), póg-pa (pog pa) to be struck by the heat, to receive a sun-stroke; also to be taken ill with dysentery, to which the Tibetans, used to the dry atmosphere of the northern Himalaya, are very liable, when during summer they venture into the southern subtropical regions; tsád-can (tshad can) hot, e.g. yul (yul); tsad-ldán (tshad ldan) prob. id.; me-búm tsád-can (me bum tshad can), Lt. a hot cupping-glass (?). 2. morbid heat of the body, fever (W. *tsan-zúg* (*tshan zug*)); tsad-pai nad (tshad pai nad) id., but also dysentery, v. above Glr., C.; tsad-pa źag-gñis-ma (tshad pa zhag gnyis ma) tertian fever Schr.; gya-tṣe' (gya tshe) Sik. Indian or jungle-fever; *roṅ-tṣe* (*rong tshe*) Sik common intermittent fever. - II. vb. Cs.: to measure, = tsád-du byéd-pa (tshad du byed pa), tsad jál-ba (tshad 'jal ba).

Cs.: 'measure, rule, model, proof, argument; logic'; tsád-ma-pa (tshad ma pa), or -mkan (-mkhan), Cs. logician, dialectitian; tsád-mai bstan-bcós (tshad mai bstan bcos) a dialectical work Pth.; tsad-ma gźuṅ (tshad ma gzhung) an original work on dialectics Cs.; tsád-ma grél-ba (tshad ma 'grel ba) commentary to it Cs.; saṅs-rgyás-kyi bka tsád-mar bźág-pa (sangs rgyas kyi bka tshad mar bzhag pa) the words of Buddha reduced to a dogmatical system (?) Pth. - tsád-ma kun-dús (tshad ma kun dus), tsád-ma sde bdun (tshad ma sde bdun) titles of books mentioned by Was.

1. a root = tsa (tsha) in tsá-ba (tsha ba) hot, warm C. and B.; tsán-mo (tshan mo) (*tsém-mo* (*tshem mo*)), in W. *tsán-te* (*tshan te*), e.g. with ču (chu), *ču tsém-mo* (*chu tshem mo*) C., *ču-tsán* (*chu tshan*) W., hot water Dzl., warm water Lt.; zan-drón tsán-mo (zan dron tshan mo) warm food Lt.; ču-skól tsán-mo (chu skol tshan mo) boiling water Mng.; *ša tsó-pa tṣém-mo* (*sha tsho pa tshem mo*) boiled meat, in Lhasa brought warm to the market; *tṣeṅ-dí táṅ-wa* (*tsheng di tang wa*) C. to proceed capitally against, ni. f.; tsán-te (tshan te) sharp, biting, pungent, W. also sbst.: spice, esp. red pepper. - tsan-źug (tshan zhug) W. fever. - tsan-ró (tshan ro) Sch.: 'hot, the sensation of heat'. - 2. = tsá-bo (tsha bo): *pa-tsén* (*pha tshen*) cousin by the father's, *ma-tsén* (*ma tshen*) by the mother's side C.; pa-tsán (pha tshan) also = pa-spún (pha spun); kú-tsán (khu tshan) v. kú-bo (khu bo). - 3. series, order, class, sde-tsán (sde tshan) id.; bźi-tsán (bzhi tshan) a class or collection of four things, tetrad Gram.; drug-tsán-du sdébs-pa (drug tshan du sdebs pa) to put together in classes of six Mil.; don-tsán (don tshan) Tar. 96, 14, a certain class of ideas, range of thoughts Schf. - 4. as termination of some collective nouns: gnyen-tsán (gnyen tshan), nye-tsán (nye tshan) kindred, relations, nye-tsán bdúd-kyi bśol-débs yin (nye tshan bdud kyi bshol debs yin) Mil.; blón-po-tsan lṅá-po (blon po tshan lnga po) the five embassies, ni f. Glr. - 5. naṅ-tsan (nang tshan) part, of a country, district, Tar. 90, 20. - 6. čos-tsan (chos tshan) any treatise under a distinct head or title in a volume Cs. - 7. difference Sch.; le-tsan (le tshan) different divisions, sections, chapters. - 8. much, large, copious, great, *ka tsan čiṅ-te* (*ka tshan ching te*) W. much deep snow; tsan-čé-ba (tshan che ba), tsan-čen (tshan chen) very much, a great deal, las ṅán ni tsan-čé (las ngan ni tshan che) a great many bad actions Thgr.; lo tsan-čé-ba (lo tshan che ba) a plentiful harvest, rich crop Glr.; hence tsán-po (tshan po) a dignitary, grandee Pth.; kams-tsán (khams tshan), 1. prefect of a provincial association, in large convents, such as Sera and others. 2. association, club.

(cf. tsúb-pa (tshub pa)), representative, com. tsáb-po (tshab po) C., W., *kó-la tsáb-po yod* (*kho la tshab po yod*) he has got a representative, proxy; in reference to a thing: equivalent, substitute, des tsab ruṅ (des tshab rung) it may be replaced by this, tsab ruṅ tsam-mo (tshab rung tsam mo) this may perhaps be used as a substitute Wdn.; *táb-pìi tsab cè-ce* (*tab pii tshab ce ce*) W. to use as a mop; ṅas tsab byao (ngas tshab byao) I shall supply his place Tar.; tsáb-tu (tshab tu) instead of, in the place of, már-mei (mar mei) instead of a lamp, for a lamp Glr.; in W. *tsáb-la* (*tshab la*) very common. Chiefly in compounds: sku-tsáb (sku tshab) resp. = tsáb-po (tshab po) representative of a superior, hence, as may be the case, vice-roy, delegate, commissioner, agent. - rgyal-tsáb (rgyal tshab) v. rgyál-ba (rgyal ba). - do-tsab (do tshab) Schr. prob. = tsáb-po (tshab po) = sku-tsáb (sku tshab). - rta-tsáb (rta tshab) a thing given as an equivalent for a horse Cs. - nor-tsáb (nor tshab) goods serving as a compensation for something else. - pa-tsáb (pha tshab) guardian, trustee. - bla-tsáb (bla tshab) representative of a Lama, Vice-Lama. - bu-tsáb (bu tshab) adopted child, foster-child. - mi-tsáb (mi tshab) Schr. negociator, mediator; hostage (?).
